aba: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Abé.
aback: adverb. Towards the back or rear; backwards.
arak: noun. A clear, unsweetened aniseed -flavoured alcoholic drink, produced and consumed primarily in the Levant.
arb: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Standard Arabic.
arc: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aramaic.

award: noun. A judgment, sentence, or final decision.

back: adjective. At or near the rear.
bad: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Banda languages.
bar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bavarian.
bard: noun. A professional poet and singer, like among the ancient Celts, whose occupation was to compose and sing verses in honor of the heroic
bark: verb. To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).
bawd: noun. One who keeps a brothel, or procures women for prostitution; a procurer or madam.
bra: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Braj.
brad: noun. A thin, small nail, with a slight projection at the top on one side instead of a head, or occasionally with a small domed head,

cad: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Caddo.

crab: noun. Any crustacean of the infraorder Brachyura, having five pairs of legs, the foremost of which are in the form of claws, and a carapace.
craw: noun. The stomach of an animal.
dab: verb. To press lightly in a repetitive motion with a soft object without rubbing.

dark: adjective. Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.

drab: noun. A fabric, usually of thick cotton or wool, having a dull brownish yellow, dull grey, or dun colour.
draw: verb. Senses relating to exerting force or pulling.
drawback: noun. A disadvantage; something that detracts or takes away.

rad: symbol. radian, unit of measure for angle

war: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Waray-Waray.
